programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A mother worries that her daughters ghost hunting has attracted paranormal trouble when she hears strange noises coming from the basement and their pets turn violent

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps newly-married couple John and Samantha. The pair are having trouble taming the latters cat, Puma, which seems resentful of John in particular

programme.desc: The work of pioneering engineers seeking to solve spaces biggest mysteries via access to an international range of institutions, including Nasa, the European Space Agency and CERN

programme.desc: The science behind amazing animal facts, including how a lizard can walk on water. Plus, the question of whether jellyfish live for ever

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A bird expert reveals how a barn owl catches prey it cannot even see. Plus, what makes octopuses masters of disguise

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: After 25 years in a tiny clinic, the team moves to a new building with twice the space. Dr Jeff also makes a special connection with a dog owner

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tiana is shocked by the condition of a stray dog after a challenging rescue. Tia and the family tackle their messy pigeon problem

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy examines the psychological patterns of feline Dexter, who has developed a killing streak. Plus a cat given as a birthday present proves a bad omen

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Greg and Dina, who are worried about their vicious cat. Plus, Chris and Tenille baulk at moving in together because of the latters hostile feline

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson constructs a thrill-seekers dream treetop escape in Washington, complete with a bouncy-bridge entryway and a zip-line through the forest

programme.title: Wild Dubai
programme.desc: Behind modern Dubai lies a hidden treasure - an incredible wildlife that speaks of a timeless connection to the desert, the sea and the history and tradition of its people

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.sub-title: Backwards Birds and Slimy Sea Creatures
programme.desc: High-speed footage reveals how the hummingbird can fly backwards, and a look at the hagfish, which is unlikely to win any underwater beauty contests

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.sub-title: Puppy Dog Tails and Crocodile Jaws
programme.desc: What a dog is saying when it wags its tail, and the secret behind a crocodiles powerful jaws - and how to escape it

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: In the Madagascan rainforest, the team searches for a nearly invisible creature, the panther chameleon. When testing, the reptile seems to defy the laws of physics

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: Bradley and Billy discover that the Yucatan thorn forest is the ideal home for the tough spiny-tailed iguana, but a perilous place for thin-skinned humans

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: Billy gets overambitious while conducting a late barter, as the Browns try to secure their winter stores. Plus, a rogue bear plays havoc at the settlement and a nearby logging camp

programme.title: Tanked

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Atz Lee and Jane follow the first salmon run of the season, while Otto and Eivin fix the homesteads fences to contain their herd of cows

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The anglers follow a lead to a remote river in Greenland, believed to hold huge Arctic char. They also investigate a myth of Atlantic salmon occupying a lone river

programme.title: Sharks and the City: New York
programme.desc: Biologist Craig OConnell finds out whether the return of seals to the New York City harbour could also turn around the areas declining great white sharks numbers

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Mireya and Ronny make a powerful discovery in the area around the nest. Meanwhile, Bryce suspects Bigfoot is emoting infrasound to repel the team from its territory

programme.title: Lair of the Killer Crocs
programme.desc: The number of crocodiles in Australia has boomed - and they are getting bigger and more deadly. This documentary investigates attempts to relocate them

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How does a fish make a weapon out of water? Why do turtles always return to the same beach to lay their eggs? Why do rhinos all poop in the same place?

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Host and actor Mario Lopez helps the ATM team transform a 1990s coin-operated video-game console into an interactive aquarium that features an exercise bike

programme.title: Neil Sedaka Says: All You Need Is the Music
programme.desc: A one-man show from 1983 in which the late American singer-songwriter performs a diverse range of numbers from his catalogue, from the rock n roll era to a more lyrical age. Songs include Solitaire, Bad Blood, The Hungry Years, Laughter in the Rain and New York City Blues

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 11 June 1999, featuring Backstreet Boys, 21st Century Girls, Another Level, The Chemical Brothers, Cher, Feeder, Red Hot Chili Peppers and Baz Luhrmann

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 18 June 1999, featuring The Wiseguys, Shania Twain, Madonna, Cartoons, Tatyana Ali, Cher, Lauryn Hill and S Club 7

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Presenters Tony Blackburn and Gary Davies introduce an edition from March 17 1983, featuring Bananarama, the Style Council, Ultravox, Joan Armatrading, Bonnie Tyler and Bucks Fizz

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Janice Long and Simon Bates present an edition first aired on 20 March 1986, featuring performances by Jim Diamond, Pet Shop Boys, Cliff Richard and the Young Ones, the Real Thing, Diana Ross and Mr Mister

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the beauty of butterflies close up, and revealing how they inspire the images of street artist Nick Walker and the performances of burlesque dancer Vicky Butterfly. The programme also raises the question of whether a passion for these elegant insects could help people preserve much-loved landscapes. Narrated by Imelda Staunton

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: A 1977 edition of the old-time music hall programme, chaired by Leonard Sachs. Mike Reid, the Beverley Sisters, Valerie Monese, Graham Clark and members of the Players Theatre, London, entertain an audience from the stage of Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth disguises herself as a victim of domestic abuse in an attempt to find out who is threatening the future of a womens refuge. Hettys quest to find the leak opens her eyes to the terrifying realities of spousal abuse, and results in a gripping conclusion as she leads the women in defending their sanctuary against a violent motorcycle gang

programme.title: La Chimera (2023)
programme.desc: Just out of jail, English archaeologist Arthur reconnects with his wayward crew of accomplices, a happy-go-lucky collective of grave-robbers who survive by looting Etruscan tombs and fencing the ancient treasures they dig up. Comedy adventure, starring Josh OConnor, Carol Duarte, Vincenzo Nemolato and Isabella Rossellini. In Italian, English, French and Brazilian Portuguese

programme.title: Parkinson
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson interviews Alistair Cooke, the journalist and broadcaster best known for his long-running radio programme Letter from America. First broadcast in 1980

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daisy tries to encourage Onslow to notice her by picking up a toyboy in the hope of making him jealous. Meanwhile, Hyacinth forces Elizabeth and Richard to help her organise a social occasion at the church hall. Patricia Routledge and Judy Cornwell star

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy is sent to speak to the father of the boy bullying his godson - and discovers bad behaviour runs in the family

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth disguises herself as a victim of domestic abuse in an attempt to find out who is threatening the future of a womens refuge. Hettys quest to find the leak opens her eyes to the terrifying realities of spousal abuse, and results in a gripping conclusion as she leads the women in defending their sanctuary against a violent motorcycle gang

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: A 1977 edition of the old-time music hall programme, chaired by Leonard Sachs. Mike Reid, the Beverley Sisters, Valerie Monese, Graham Clark and members of the Players Theatre, London, entertain an audience from the stage of Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the beauty of butterflies close up, and revealing how they inspire the images of street artist Nick Walker and the performances of burlesque dancer Vicky Butterfly. The programme also raises the question of whether a passion for these elegant insects could help people preserve much-loved landscapes. Narrated by Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Writer, photographer and broadcaster Lucinda Lambton explores the architecture of County Durham, where she grew up fascinated with the landscape, with visits to Durham Cathedral, Beamish Open Air Museum and Lambton Castle

programme.title: Poems in Their Place: WB Yeats
programme.desc: Seamus Heaney presents poems by his fellow Irish poet, visiting Coole Park and Thoor Ballylee Castle in Co Galway. First broadcast in 1982

programme.title: The Life and Loves of Oscar Wilde
programme.desc: A candid portrait of Oscar Wilde and his remarkable family, including revelations by his grandson Merlin Holland and Lady Alice Douglas, great-grandniece of Wildes lover Lord Alfred Bosie Douglas. First aired in 1995

programme.title: The Read
programme.sub-title: The Picture of Dorian Gray: The Read with Luke Thompson
programme.desc: Actor Luke Thompson reads from Oscar Wildes only novel, The Picture of Dorian Gray. A young mans wish that his portrait would bear the marks of time and sin while he remains forever young is mysteriously granted, leading him to live a life of pleasure under the influence of the hedonistic Lord Henry Wotton. But as time goes on, the portrait reveals the consequences of his cruelty and vices

programme.title: The Master Builder
programme.desc: Max Fabers 1958 adaptation of Ibsens play set in a Norwegian home in the late 19th-century. Starring Donald Wolfit and Mai Zetterling

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe shows what can be learned from the ways in which animals adapt to changes in the world around them

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner

programme.title: The Life and Loves of Oscar Wilde
programme.desc: A candid portrait of Oscar Wilde and his remarkable family, including revelations by his grandson Merlin Holland and Lady Alice Douglas, great-grandniece of Wildes lover Lord Alfred Bosie Douglas. First aired in 1995

programme.title: The Read
programme.sub-title: The Picture of Dorian Gray: The Read with Luke Thompson
programme.desc: Actor Luke Thompson reads from Oscar Wildes only novel, The Picture of Dorian Gray. A young mans wish that his portrait would bear the marks of time and sin while he remains forever young is mysteriously granted, leading him to live a life of pleasure under the influence of the hedonistic Lord Henry Wotton. But as time goes on, the portrait reveals the consequences of his cruelty and vices

The advocate claims the enfant terrible was switched in the maternity ward with another child, leaving Gomez devastated at the prospect of losing his little girl. Animated family comedy, with the voices of Oscar Isaac and Charlize Theron and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the work of people striving to protect Asias endangered wildlife, and what routes conservation could take in the future. In Japan, scientists take pregnant sharks, killed accidentally in fishing nets, and rescue their unborn young in an incubating system that simulates a womb. The programme also visits a village that used to be a hotbed of poaching and is now a centre for bird tourism

programme.title: Michael McIntyres The Wheel

programme.title: The Weakest Link

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: If You Say It Out Loud, Its Real
programme.desc: Hurt by Annas absence, Rachel becomes even more unsettled when Claire accidentally lets something slip. Maggie receives big news of her own and later crosses paths with Luke, who is still shaken by his last encounter with Rachel. At the flat, Anna is caught off guard by an unexpected visit from Janie, who arrives with startling news

programme.desc: Nick Hewer, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Scarlett Moffatt, Monty Panesar, Louisa Clein and Shazia Mirza are on their final day in Londonderry, when the delayed seventh participant arrives. Jetting in from the Paralympics in Japan, table tennis star Will Bayley completes the pilgrim band. Eventually they leave Northern Ireland behind them and set sail across the North Channel to western Scotland and join the Kintyre Way. Life on the pilgrimage gets tough as the celebrities are challenged both physically and spiritually, struggling with lack of sleep and difficult questions about faith

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A plot of land in Leeds is a potential goldmine, that is if the new owner can obtain planning permission. Dion Dublin likes whats on offer at a bungalow in Derby, and Tommy Walsh thinks the layout at a south west London flat needs a reconfiguration

programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer help two teams look for items of value in Shrewsbury, Shropshire. Christina Trevanion oversees proceedings and also learns the centuries-old skill of clay pipe-making at the Broseley Pipeworks Museum

programme.title: Crookhaven
programme.desc: Rivalries intensify during the Break-In Challenge. Meanwhile, Amira deals with the consequences of her brothers betrayal, and Gabriel makes a shocking discovery

programme.desc: Jill Scott and La Voix take a look back at the highlights of this years charity extravaganza. programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A look at life after dark on the North York Moors. At the Dark Skies Festival, Margherita Taylor finds out how light pollution is affecting nocturnal wildlife, while Sammi Kinghorn meets the people gathering vital dark-sky data and installing wildlife-friendly lighting to help protect the night. Plus, Adam Henson meets apprentices at a cattle mart meeting

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the ruined Beaumaris Castle on the Island of Anglesey, where valuable finds include a copy of Winnie the Pooh that may be a first edition, a rare ballerina brooch, and a wooden chair featuring carvings by Yorkshire craftsman Robert Mouseman Thompson. Fiiona also discovers why the castle was never completed by King Edward I, and is challenged to a game of Basic, Better, Best by Lisa Lloyd with a collection of unusual toys.

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: In the wake of a family tragedy, the Bennet household is thrown into disarray. Mary faces an uncertain future, until her aunt and uncle offer her the chance of a fresh start. At first, Mary struggles to adjust to her new life, but she begins to realise that she can have a life away from the watchful eye of her mother

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary is finding her footing in London, but overhears something which knocks her newfound confidence. Mr Hayward introduces her to poetry, but their budding connection is complicated by Mr Haywards engagement. As Mary wrestles with troubling new feelings, she finds herself caught between duty, a new friendship and the possibility of love

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey searches for one suspect, whilst Frank Napier deals with another. As the evidence mounts and Carey is forced to avert another catastrophe, she begins to fear she might have made a terrible mistake. Piersons first day on the job gets ever more challenging, but as the net tightens for James Whitlock, it looks like SO15 might have made a breakthrough in the case

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Newcastle United v Sunderland at St James Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Nottingham Forest at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Sunderland defeated their rivals 1-0 at Stadium of Light in December in the first Tyne-Wear derby to take place in the league since the 2015/16 campaign. Spurs and Forest are both embroiled in a relegation battle, but the latter were in good form in the reverse fixture, prevailing 3-0. Plus, Aston Villa v West Ham United and a round-up of yesterdays goals

programme.title: The Womens Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekends matches in the Super League, including Arsenal v West Ham United, Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur, and Manchester United v Everton

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: The presenter is joined by comedian and award-winning podcaster Joanne McNally, who speaks to Claudia about her record-breaking stand-up tour, Pinotphile. Bafta-nominated comedian and actor Guz Khan speaks about his new Amazon Prime series Bait, which he stars in alongside Riz Ahmed. Former One Direction star and singer-songwriter Niall Horan joins Claudia to share how he went from global boy band success to solo chart-topping artist and talk about his new album, featuring his latest single Dinner Party

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: An investigation into the issues of misogyny and toxic masculinity, to coincide with the current EastEnders storyline centred on teenager Joel Marshall, played by Max Murray

programme.title: Men of the Manosphere
programme.desc: James Blake enters the virtual world of manhood, often referred to as the manosphere, in search of status, wealth and relationships. He meets three young men who have turned to male social media influencers for guidance and instruction as they seek to better themselves and achieve success in different areas of their lives, both on and offline

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Weird Weekends
programme.desc: The sceptical reporter travels to Americas South West to find out why this dustbowl, with little to recommend it to its sparse human population, should be so irresistible to aliens, who have allegedly been visiting for more than 50 years. Along the way he meets a self-styled warrior monk who has declared war on evil extraterrestrials

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor takes Clara on her first trip into outer space, travelling to the inhabited rings surrounding the planet Akhaten. Clara befriends a young girl who has been selected to take part in a sacred ritual, while an ancient and powerful being awakens from its slumber, to feast on the lives of the planets inhabitants

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara land on a damaged Russian submarine in 1983 as it sinks into the ocean depths. To make matters worse, an alien warrior from Mars is loose on board, having escaped from a block of Arctic ice. With tempers flaring and a cargo of nuclear weapons, the whole of humanity at stake. David Warner, Liam Cunningham and Tobias Menzies guest star in an episode featuring the return of classic foes the Ice Warriors

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: The spooks thoughtless behaviour sends Alison into meltdown, prompting them to come up with a creative way to get back into her good books and show her how truly sorry they are. Mary however, believing she has done nothing wrong, refuses to take any responsibility, not after the fiery fate that befell her the last time she took the blame for something she didnt do. Alison and Mike have a last-minute cancellation for the guesthouse and decide that a relaxing day off is exactly what they need. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A shocking revelation throws the Ghosts into disarray. Kitty looks to Pat for moral support, and while he is more than happy to oblige, he finds himself in deep water trying to offer her words of comfort. Another bombshell drops when Mike realises there have been some crossed wires over an event booking and he and Alison are forced into hosting an impromptu - and ill-prepared - childrens birthday bash in order not to let two little girls down. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: A secretive phone call from a solicitor to Mrs Brown leaves her children suspecting one of them may be adopted, while Father Quinn returns from rehab and asks Agnes to help out at his annual garden party - although as she proceeds to drive him crazy with a smartphone, his recovery may be short-lived. Meanwhile, Cathy receives a blast from the past, Rory and Dino have a bust-up, and Mark and Betty deliver bad news - they are leaving for Australia right away. Domestic comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: The Browns are excited as preparations for Rory and Dinos big day are in full swing - although Agness mood is dampened when she meets flamboyant celebrity wedding planner La La Doggy. The foul-mouthed matriarch also clashes with Father Damien when he raises religious concerns about the ceremony - and to top it all off, she gets more than she bargained for when she invites Cathys boyfriends parents over for the evening. Hit comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll, with Jennifer Gibney, Rory Cowan and Conor Moloney

programme.title: Alan Partridge: Alpha Papa (2013)
programme.desc: The hapless local radio DJ ends up in the centre of a siege when colleague Pat Farrell gets fired by the stations new owners, only to respond by returning to the studio with a shotgun and taking hostages. The police enlist Alans aid as a negotiator, giving him the chance to revive his own flagging public profile. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Colm Meaney and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz tries to ease his guilt by doing something special for Donna, and Janet throws a party in honour of Louises graduation. Meanwhile, Jonny and Kate are more concerned with finding somewhere private. Will Mellor and Natalie Casey star

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janets new boss makes her life a misery, while Gaz resolves to split the lovely Kate from Jonny, who in turn receives an ultimatum to dump his friends or face a return to being single. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith and Will Mellor. First broadacst in 2004

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donnas tutor invites her to have a little extracurricular fun, but she is shocked when it takes place on his sofa. Gaz experiences problems in the bedroom and Janet urges a reluctant Jonny to sample her latest pasty recipe

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna enjoys some filthy brunching with her tutor, while Louise gets Jonny into trouble with the police. Gaz solves his bedroom problem and Janet becomes a vociferous advocate of adventurous sex

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Comedy about five talented black teenagers at a prestigious boarding school. As the boarders gear up for their final term, a shocking act of vandalism throws everything into chaos. With the school on lockdown, Toby has to find a way to break out and see Abby. Leah is under suspicion for the school destruction, and Femi is distracted by a mystery girl. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. Omar is in denial, and Toby is distracted by Abbys dangerous games. Violence erupts on the hockey pitch, and the students bright futures feel further away than ever

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: An investigation into the issues of misogyny and toxic masculinity, to coincide with the current EastEnders storyline centred on teenager Joel Marshall, played by Max Murray

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Its Mothers Day in Albert Square, and Lauren and Peter prepare to celebrate with the Beales at No 45. An upset Linda prepares to celebrate the day in the Vic with Elaine as Johnny is still distant with her, but things soon go from bad to worse when shes forced to invite Bea. Jean arrives back in Walford, but its clear there is still friction between her and Zoe over Jasmine

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lauren gets off to a good start as she successfully sells one of Marks cars. Denise and Kim jump into protection mode when they hear a woman is asking questions about Jasmine. Vinny attempts to tell Suki about Pennys pregnancy, but Suki shuts down the conversation before he can reveal the truth

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Josh and Sandra join Denise and Kim for a drink in the Albert as he searches for answers about Jasmine, but Denise soon puts her foot in it by mentioning Zoe. Lauren and Peter meet a mum whose baby is also visually impaired, to discuss a specialist nursery, but the pair bristle when they realise the cost. Elsewhere, Billy grows concerned about Beas obsession with his family after speaking to Harvey, and Suki comes to blows with Penny and Vinny about the pregnancy

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise and Kim buoy Patrick over Josh and encourage him to write a letter about his life story. The pair head back to Sandras to deliver it, and she encourages Josh to read Patricks letter. Max makes an effort with Linda, and Billys approach causes upset

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: The decluttering team land in south London to help single mum Almarie and her 10-year-old daughter Marie overhaul their three-bedroom semi. With Staceys guidance, Almarie sorts through the belongings of her husband Marcus for the first time since his sudden death three years ago, creating space to honour old memories and create new ones. Back at the house Rob builds a stage for budding performer Marie and transforms an outhouse into an art studio

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Femi is feeling the pressure as he tries to make a good impression with Cheddars dad, who gives him a daring proposal - but Pixie proves very distracting. programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: BBC Medical Editor Fergus Walsh reports on the future of assisted dying in the UK, travelling to Western Australia, where voluntary assisted dying has been available since 2021 to meet one of the doctors who pioneered it, and to a patient preparing for an assisted death. He also talks to leading supporters and opponents in the UK, and visits a hospice to talk to patients at the end of their lives

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Wales must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee, Phil Wang and guest judge Sally El Hosaini, the Bafta-nominated director of acclaimed film The Swimmers. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Period drama based on Janice Hadlows novel, telling the story of the overlooked middle sister in Pride and Prejudice, both during the events of Jane Austens classic novel and beyond. The Meryton assembly brings Marys first taste of romance, but also leaves her forced to decide between trying to please her family and forging a path of her own. Starring Ella Bruccoleri and Richard E Grant

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: After disappointment at the ball, Mary sets aside hopes of finding a match and devotes herself to more serious pursuits. But Marys resolve is tested when Mr Bennets distant cousin, Mr Collins, visits Longbourn with a surprising proposal. Mary contemplates a decision which could save her family from ruin, but is she really willing to suppress her true feelings to secure her familys future?

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.sub-title: Battling Islamic State Group in Somalia
programme.desc: The team follows Puntland forces on the front line in far-eastern Somalia, examining how the Islamic State group has expanded its influence across Africa. Plus, Kalkidan Yibeltal has rare access to Jonglei state in South Sudan, where renewed conflict threatens a fragile peace accord

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: A salesman visits convinces the Piris to buy extra flippers. The penguins discover that their purchases are duds but find a clever new use for them

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: A giant beetle steals DORISs brain, thinking its a nut, so Cosima and Big Lizard give chase through the forest

programme.desc: After the Yetis house is destroyed, the Lemmings are quick to blame Grizzy

programme.desc: The Dino Bros return to join a real school sports day, and Paralympian Hannah Cockroft joins Callum to judge. Joe and Adam build the worlds longest marble run

programme.sub-title: Sabre, Legend, Cyclone, Nitro v the Hotel!
programme.desc: Nitro, Cyclone, Legend and Sabre compete at a hotel check-in desk as they dare each other with outrageous instructions for the chance to win or tap out and risk losing

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The show becomes Red Peter for Red Nose Day. The stars of Gladiators: Epic Pranks and Saturday Mash-Up join in a last-to-laugh challenge, and Joel attempts springboard diving to help raise money

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day Two Morning Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day two at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The mens heptathlon continues with the 60m hurdles and pole vault, while the heats of the mens 60m hurdles also take place, before the session concludes with the mens and womens 800m semi-finals. The other notable event is the final of the mixed 4x400m relay, which is making its debut at this championship

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Adam Partridge and Christina Trevanion value objects at Hereford Cathedral, where one item goes over the £1,000 mark at auction. Paul Martin explores his craft-making skills as he tries to make a traditional wooden stool

programme.title: The Third Man (1949)
programme.desc: American writer Holly Martins goes to post-Second World War Vienna at the invitation of his old friend Harry Lime, only to be told that Lime has been killed by a car while crossing the street. However, the author finds the circumstances surrounding his death fail to add up and goes in search of the truth - but is unprepared for the ugly secrets he uncovers. Carol Reeds thriller, written by Graham Greene, starring Joseph Cotten, Orson Welles, Alida Valli and Trevor Howard

programme.title: Super League Live
programme.desc: Warrington Wolves v Castleford Tigers (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the top-flight match from Halliwell Jones Stadium. The Wolves scored six tries in a 34-24 victory when the teams met here last season, with Josh and Jake Thewlis, Rodrick Tai, and Arron Lindop all crossing the line in the first half, and Ben Currie and Stefan Ratchford doing likewise in the second, with the latter also making his 350th appearance for Warrington

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day Two Evening Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day two at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The mens heptathlon concludes with the 1,000m, which last year saw Norways Sander Skotheim claim the gold medal with a narrow victory over Johannes Erm, despite the Estonians victory in this event, as he had to settle for silver. Elsewhere, gold medals will also be awarded in eight disciplines, including the mens and womens 400m and 3,000m, the mens 60m hurdles, and womens 60m

programme.title: Comic Reliefs Hits and Hidden Gems at the BBC
programme.desc: Scott Mills looks at the greatest musical moments from the long-running charity, including chart-topping performances by Adele, One Direction and the Spice Girls, as well as rarely seen highlights from Sam Smith and John Legend, Justin Bieber, Marti Pellow, McFly - and Helen Mirren and Robbie Coltrane

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A celebration of the biggest songs from 1987. Performers include Rick Astley, Whitney Houston, TPau, Mel and Kim, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Eric B and Rakim, Pet Shop Boys, Alison Moyet and Boy George

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A celebration of the best of the years programmes, including performances by Yazz, SExpress, Bomb the Bass, Kylie Minogue, Jason Donovan, Bros, Tiffany, Womack and Womack, Enya, Maxi Priest and Everything But the Girl. Plus, the Proclaimers, Neneh Cherry, Tanita Tikaram, Eurythmics, Ofra Haza and Fairground Attraction

programme.title: Aftersun (2022)
programme.desc: Sophie reflects on the shared joy and private melancholy of a holiday she took with her father 20 years earlier. Memories real and imagined fill the gaps between as she tries to reconcile the father she knew with the man she didnt. Drama, starring Frankie Corio and Paul Mescal

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: More video evidence of the attack emerges. Careys assertion that an insidious conspiracy is unfolding at the heart of the establishment gains little traction, so she takes matters into her own hands and seeks an unlikely ally in Gemma Garland. Meanwhile, Noah Pierson finds his first day at work more surprising than he ever expected

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: The bottled prophets are looking unwell and Michael still hasnt asked his questions. Brian is causing havoc at the nursing home and warns Michael and Kacey that time is of the essence, but on their return, all hell has broken out in Marvin Gardens., with the cul-de-sac crammed full of vehicles, strangers and irate neighbours

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: Civil servant Paul hosts his hike along the stunning Alnwick coast with a taste of traditional Northumbria

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Anita Rani head to Devon for an edition devoted to lambing. From scanning ewes for pregnancy, to the rush of new births, and bottle-feeding vulnerable lambs through their early days, Adam and Anita look at all stages of lambing, following the teamwork, care and expertise that bring new life safely into the world in a county shaped by generations of sheep farming

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day Three Morning Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on the third and final day at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The womens pentathlon gets under way with the 60m hurdles, high jump and shot put, and the finals of the womens long jump and mens shot put also take place

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Just one positive TB test result could mean quarantine for Lucy and Adams dairy herd and disaster for their move to Scotland. Gayle is banned from the lambing shed to protect her unborn child from an infection transmitted through sheep after birth, which puts extra pressure on Andrew and his dad Terrance. Ally passes on his lambing knowledge to his chidren Bethany and Noah, hoping to increase the numbers of young crofters on Lewis

programme.title: Womens Super League
programme.desc: Leicester City v Aston Villa (Kick-off 2.45pm). All the action from the match at King Power Stadium. The reverse fixture in October saw the teams draw 0-0, but both teams have struggled so far this season and will each be determined to take all three points on this occasion and boost their chances of avoiding relegation

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day Three Evening Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on the third and final day at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The womens pentathlon concludes with the long jump and 800m, with Saga Vanninen of Finland the reigning champion after beating Irelands Kate OConnor into second place last year in China. Medals are also awarded in the womens pole vault, 1500m, 800m, 60m hurdles and 4x400m, and the mens 1500m, long jump, 800m and 4x400m

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Walmer, the crew responds to reports of a major incident involving a small boat in the English Channel, on arrival they are faced with mass casualties in freezing water. A 75-year-old skipper and his two young grandsons are aboard a yacht thats lost all power and is drifting towards a wind farm. The north Wales crew must race to rescue the vessel and its passengers before its too late

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: At 1am in Cumbria, a 999 call reports a gang breaking into a supermarket cash machine using high-powered cutting tools. They work quickly, escaping with thousands before police arrive. Its the second ATM targeted in the region within 24 hours. Seventy miles away, news of the raids is shared with West Midlands Police, who are already pursuing a prolific gang linked to similar attacks nationwide. As the task force builds intelligence, Cumbria Police lead the forensic investigation, until a third raid heightens the urgency of the case

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.sub-title: Olesya Zdorovetska, Ronán Ó Snodaigh and Myles OReilly
programme.desc: Three artists set forth on a journey of musical collaboration. Ronán Ó Snodaigh is a world-renowned Irish songwriter, composer, musician, and poet known for his revolutionary bodhrán playing style and being the frontman of Kíla. Myles OReilly is a musician and music-documentary filmmaker with a love for collaboration and experimentation. Joining them is Olesya Zdorovetska, a Ukrainian vocalist, musician, and composer whose work has been described as an emotional and intellectual explosion

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.desc: Brìghde Chaimbeul is a leading purveyor of experimental Celtic music and of the Scottish smallpipes. Joining Brìghde on this musical exploration of the meeting points between traditional Irish and Scottish music is Jack Talty, a concertina player, composer and producer from the traditional Irish music hotbed of Co Clare

programme.title: Written by Us
programme.desc: Award-winning concert filmed at Belfasts Grand Opera House celebrating the global impact of songs written by musicians from Northern Ireland. Host Eamonn McCrystal brings together Rachel Tucker, Brian Kennedy, Keith and Kristyn Getty and May McFettridge for an evening of music. From Van Morrison to Snow Patrol and Jimmy Kennedy, the show revisits internationally recognised hits, including Moondance and South of the Border, revealing the Northern Irish writers behind songs that became part of a global soundtrack

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Inside the Rage Machine
programme.desc: The BBCs social media investigations correspondent Marianna Spring uncovers the inside story of how social media algorithms designed to connect people have been helping to tear them apart. Insider testimony and whistleblower documents expose a machine thriving on outrage and division as part of a business model, with radicalisation, real-world violence and fractured societies some of the consequences of a system built to shape how users think, feel and see the world

programme.title: Barra on the Foyle
programme.desc: Barra Best travels along the Foyle river network, criss-crossing his way across the many rivers and streams that make up its great catchment

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, with Health Secretary Wes Streeting; Conservative Shadow Work and Pensions Secretary Helen Whately; President of the Liberal Democrats Josh Babarinde; former leader of the Green Party Caroline Lucas and Reform UKs Head of Policy James Orr facing questions from the audience in Croydon

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: Documentary about the fate of photojournalists John Cantlie and James Foley, who were kidnapped in Syria in 2012. The pair had gone out to the Middle East to chase a scoop about foreign fighters, only for their reporting to draw the attention of extremists - and what began as a tense investigation spiralled into a dangerous encounter

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Spring is in full stride at Longmeadow, and Monty Don gets straight to work, bringing new borders to life, reviving winter-worn houseplants and priming his pelargoniums for a standout summer. Joe Swift enjoys access to the private garden of renowned designer Mary Keen, and at RHS Garden Rosemoor, Carol Klein reveals how fruit and veg can deliver both flavour and flair - no matter the size of your plot

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Gary from north Wales brings in an electric guitar that once belonged to his father Eddie Clayton - who once played in a skiffle group with none other than a pre-Beatles Ringo Starr. The instrument is now damaged and no longer working, so restorer Julyan Wallis gets to work. Other items are an exquisite bangle worn by the owners grandmother, who left Guyana for the UK in 1962 as part of the Windrush generation, a familys rediscovered set of Kent peg tiles and a leather tallit bag - a Jewish family heirloom passed down through generations when a boy celebrates his bar mitzvah

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Newcastle United v Sunderland at St James Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Nottingham Forest at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Sunderland defeated their rivals 1-0 at Stadium of Light in December in the first Tyne-Wear derby to take place in the league since the 2015/16 campaign. Spurs and Forest are both embroiled in a relegation battle, but the latter were in good form in the reverse fixture, prevailing 3-0. Plus, Aston Villa v West Ham United and a round-up of yesterdays goals

programme.desc: A farmer discovers a cow mysteriously mutilated, a man is repeatedly abducted from his bed, and an astronomers telescope intercepts a mysterious message from outer space

programme.title: Weird or What? With William Shatner
programme.desc: Decoding ancient Mayan texts, a physicist predicts a cataclysm will destroy the world. Plus, a Nasa scientist makes an incredible alien discovery, and one of the worlds greatest minds makes a startling prediction about a sinister robot takeover

programme.desc: A woman who claims to have encountered shadow people and a man who claims to have used teleportation, travelling from New Jersey to New Mexico in the blink of an eye

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Veteran actor William Shatner explores the Earth and beyond, in search of the universes most fascinating, strange and inexplicable mysteries. This opening episode documents unidentified objects witnessed by military pilots, extraterrestrial probes flying through the solar system and a potential alien home world that may be hidden in the darkness of space

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Veteran actor William Shatner examines the possibility of contact with otherworldly intelligent life, and how man should react to and handle such a situation. Plus, the practicality of man travelling beyond the moon

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: William Shatner addresses Earth itself, which remains in large part a mystery to the eight billion people who live on it. He analyses how Earth came to exist, and the secrets of its survival and contemplates how long humans have left to call it home

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Humanity has worshipped the Sun since the beginning of time, and yet even with modern technology, so little is known about it. William Shatner considers whether unlocking the mysteries of the Sun can be the difference between life and death for human civilization

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: As the next era of space exploration begins it must be analysed what humans will find when reaching other planets, as well as the dangers faced when trying to conquer new frontiers. Presented by William Shatner

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.sub-title: The Life and Death of the Sun
programme.desc: Humanity has worshipped the Sun since the beginning of time, and yet even with the help of modern technology there is much that is still not known about it. Veteran actor William Shatner explores its creation and finite status. He outlines what is likely to happen as it nears its life-end

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: How, at a mysterious site in the Gobi Desert, experts discovered a monster graveyard - a groundbreaking find containing treasures of alien beasts

programme.title: What on Earth?

programme.desc: Actor Laurence Fishburne examines the possible causes of mysterious vanishings in the Bermuda Triangle, a 500,000 square-mile area of the Atlantic where ships, planes and unsuspecting travellers have been lost without a trace

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Hunt for Stolen Nazi Treasure
programme.desc: During the Second World War, the Nazis stole vast amounts of art, gold, silver and currency, and stashed it across Europe. While some of the plunder has been recovered, much more is still missing nearly 80 years later

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: The story of Noahs ark, the biblical tale in which God sends a flood to wipe out nearly every living thing on Earth, except for Noah, his family and the animals he saves in his fabled ark. This episode details the search for the ark

programme.desc: For four centuries the name Ivan the Terrible has conjured up the image of a vicious, cruel despot. This documentary investigates whether this is a purely Western view. In his own land, Russias first tsar is remembered as a statesman who held the country together through difficult times - and perhaps was not so terrible after all

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Ancient practices such as prayer, blood sports, voodoo and exorcism are often thought to be relics of bygone superstition, though there is evidence they have an impact on the physical world. Is it possible that the mind and body can use the power of ritual to tap into hidden forces that lie just beyond the grasp of conventional explanation?

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Perhaps the greatest unexplained mystery of all is what happens after death. programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis which sees a team of five friends from Southampton compete to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five more sellers bring their items to the Bidding Room. Sophie brings in a rugby shirt from the film Chariots of Fire and James hopes to sell a truck door. Next up, John and Nicky present a coin operated ride-on train and car to the dealers and George brings in a 20th century card table with a secret drawer. Plus, Barry comes in with some stained-glass panels

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Old Man Tour
programme.desc: A chance to see a live performance recorded at Blackpools historic Grand Theatre, on what was the final night of the comedians sell-out, 158-date UK and Ireland tour. Here, Jon takes to the stage to comment - and generally complain - on topics including parenthood, Brexit and haircuts. The question of why nobody but him can correctly load a dishwasher is also raised. Alas, Jon can offer no solutions to all the problems he identifies, but nevertheless provides plenty of mirth as he fails to reach a viable conclusion to his grouses

programme.title: Sean Lock: Keep It Light
programme.desc: The 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown captain captured live on stage in a stand-up performance, talking about everything from the price of cinema food, accusations of him having a mid-life crisis, his behaviour on the internet and jewellery heists. From 2019

programme.title: Everybodys Talking About Jamie (2021)
programme.desc: Jamie New celebrates his 16th birthday with a gift of glittery red heels from his supportive mother, Margaret. Nurturing a dream of performing on stage, Jamie recruits Hugo Battersby aka drag doyenne Loco Chanelle as his mentor to step confidently into the spotlight. Comedy drama, starring Max Harwood, Richard E Grant, Sarah Lancashire, Lauren Patel and Shobna Gulati

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Anglers Lodge in Island Park, Idaho. Owner Dave Eby built the lodge with his own hands, but a family tragedy has caused him and wife Dede to shy away from the hotel and the community. Gordon is desperate to help mend this broken family - but is it too late to save their business?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Anglesey, where best pals Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch hope to show that Blackthorn Farm BandB, which they own, is better than any of their guests establishments. However, they find that their close friendship is scrutinised even more than their property

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The White Swan Hotel in Yeadon, West Yorkshire, is the second stop of the competition, and owners Graham and Barbara Yellow hope their basic facilities and affordable prices will please their guests. Spiritual couple Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor get good vibes from their room, Rachel Lonergan views the hosts in a new light and breakfast is well received by the contestants, despite having to pay a supplement fee

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Vegetarians Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor host at the Old Mill House BandB in Bridport, Dorset, and both come under attack at dinner when Malcolm voices his opinions on meat eating. In the morning, a meditation session gets a mixed response, and most of the guests are left cold by the vegetarian buffet breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Rachel and Dan Lonergan welcome their guests to the Brighton Inn, East Sussex, where they hope to offer a luxury, boutique experience. Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor give their room a thorough inspection, before the groups learns the art of building a human pyramid in the afternoons activity. At breakfast, they discover a lack of vegetarian alternatives, and the hosts suspect game-playing when they are shocked by some negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners come together to confront their rivals and find out how much they have been paid. Richard and Susan are keen to tackle the bathroom issues of Barbara and Graham, who bask in the comments from Malcolm and Fiona. The Old Mill House proprietors defend their vegetarian breakfast routine, but serve up snobby feedback to Rachel and Dan from the Brighton Inn

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie decide to try for a baby - but with Arthur under their feet, finding time alone proves difficult. The delivery driver keeps the old man occupied by hiring a dog walker to listen to his stories about the Second World War. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug treats Carrie to laser eye surgery for her birthday - but the side effects leave her blind for a week, and shes not happy to learn her husband chose the cheapest doctor he could find

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Dougs colleagues resent his achievement in breaking the record for perfect deliveries, while Carrie wonders why the dog walker enjoys spending time with her father. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra is too ill to take Ally to a pyjama party, so Ray reluctantly agrees to do the honours. Unfortunately it means coming face to face with Peggy, an old enemy who physically assaulted him during a charity event

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra wonders how Ray would cope if she met an untimely end, and picks an attractive friend to replace her as his new wife - but cannot help being hurt by his enthusiastic response to the idea

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra throws a bridal shower for Amy, but Marie tries to take over and monopolises the event. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Monica Horan

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Maris tries to woo Niles with anonymous presents, but Frasier thinks the gifts were intended for him and risks his latest relationship to learn the identity of his secret admirer. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niless expensive divorce leaves him penniless, forcing him to move into Frasier and Martins apartment. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: The Seal Who Came to Dinner
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier prepare for a dinner party at Mariss beach house, but a bizarre turn of events results in them having to devote all their time to disposing of a dead seal that has washed up on the shore

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa meets a fellow horse-lover, but then falls in with a circle of snooty girls, while Homer takes Marge on a romantic cruise

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Part one of two. A charismatic new preacher comes to town and shakes things up at church, but Reverend Lovejoy fights back by investigating the mans mysterious past

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Part two of two. Reverend Lovejoy discovers the real reason Bode came to Springfield and it remains to be seen if the congregation will continue to accept him

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first destination of the week is Becketts in Southsea, Portsmouth, where parking problems, a duvet disaster and a noisy night leave host Terence facing less than perfect scores

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals arrive at the Westby Guest House in Boscombe, where Duncan and Jessies strict house rules are not to everyones liking - and the breakfast is quality over quantity

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Paul and Tracey host the third visit of the week at Newell Restaurant and Rooms in Sherborne, Dorset. Terence is taken with the blissful bedding but Duncan has a substandard slumber - and a communal coffee zone proves controversial

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the final visit of the week, the BandBers embrace the great outdoors at Lydcott Glamping in Cornwall. Hosts Emma and Rich think their cabins will set them on the path to victory

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers meet up on payment day to settle their differences. In a particularly emotional discussion, there is plenty of finger pointing before the payments are revealed

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: In Hamilton, Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer dive headfirst into a domestic battleground with warring couple Scott and Jaqui. Their once-perfect four-bedroom new-build, which they bought 14 years ago when their two daughters were still at home, now feels like a misfit. With the kids now gone, the house no longer works and is causing consternation. Scott, who works from home full-time, is fiercely attached to his beloved office, double garage and the local area, and refuses to entertain the idea of leaving. Meanwhile, Jaqui is at her wits end with the cramped kitchen and lack of space for entertaining. Shes convinced their dream home is out there, and shes desperately relying on Phil to get Scott on board. Kirstie faces a Herculean challenge, working with a £60,000 Love It budget to create the extra space Jaqui craves. Meanwhile, Phil navigates treacherous waters, searching for properties to meet Jaquis high standards and somehow persuade Scott that selling up is the best option - all within a £450,000 List It budget. With emotions running high and compromises in short supply, its an epic showdown. Wholl come out on top?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Two sets of house-hunters look for properties in north London. Kirstie Allsopp is with single mum and businesswoman Digi, who has £1.25million to find the perfect place for her, daughter Emily and mum Queelan. However, it soon proves a challenge to find the family home that suits all their needs. Phil Spencer helps Rebecca and Sohail, who have raised their family in rented accommodation and now want a place of their own now. The problem is finding a place they will love when their rental is worth at least £200,000 more than their budget to buy

programme.title: Sleepless in Seattle (1993)
programme.desc: A widowed father is coping badly with his wifes death, but his son is convinced that what he really needs is some new romance in his life. He arranges for his dad to go on a radio phone-in show, and his touching story moves a reporter to respond, even though she is already engaged. She returns to her fiance, but the boy, certain she is the woman for his father, plots to bring them together. Romantic comedy, with Tom Hanks and Meg Ryan

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Race for Ancient Egypt in Colour
programme.desc: Part two of two. Howard Carter returns to Tutankhamuns tomb and the boy kings death mask captivates the world. Meanwhile, Egypts rulers insist that all treasures remain in the country. Archaeologists rush to uncover the next Tutankhamun, but nothing comes close until extraordinary treasures are uncovered at Tanis. However, as the world goes to war, the race for ancient Egypt is paused

programme.title: The Roman Empire by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: Alice heads to Tarragona, once the capital of Iberian Rome, which boasts the largest Roman forum in the world and a vast amphitheatre unearthed below a church. In Cartagena, on Spains south coast, Alice marvels at the areas subterranean riches and takes a skylift above the city to reflect on the many things her 1300-mile Roman odyssey has taught her

programme.title: Bill Baileys Vietnam
programme.desc: Bill heads north to Hanoi, Vietnams capital, to explore the countrys complex relationship with life, death and war. On the shores of the Red River Delta, Bill discovers a culture defined by spirituality, femininity and a unique tradition for honouring the dead. In celebrating those he has lost, Bill finds a way to pay homage to the most important figures in his own life

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Rogue Nation (2015)
programme.desc: The team of secret agents faces being shut down when the head of the CIA convinces the US Senate that it is too dangerous. The operatives of the Impossible Mission Force become fugitives when they defy orders and plot to bring down a shadowy society of assassins. Spy thriller sequel, starring Tom Cruise, Jeremy Renner, Rebecca Ferguson and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Infinity Pool (2023)
programme.desc: James and Em Foster are enjoying an all-inclusive beach vacation on the island of La Tolqa. However, a fatal accident exposes the resorts perverse subculture of hedonistic tourism, reckless violence and surreal horrors. Horror mystery, starring Alexander Skarsgard, Mia Goth, Cleopatra Coleman and Thomas Kretschmann

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Southbridge, Massachusetts, where he checks into the Vienna Inn, an Austrian-themed hotels and restaurant. Owners Jonathan and Lisa Krach have seen their cluttered inn acquire a negative reputation among the locals, and as a result, business isnt what it used to be. Can Gordon turn things round and keep the doors to this historic hotel open?

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: With the final getting ever closer, the handcuffed couples face a new challenge: their first holiday away together. Jonathan Ross sends them across the UK on iconic British breaks, from beautiful canal trips to camping in the wild countryside, and enjoying all the fun of the fair at the great British seaside. On neutral territory, and without friends and family around, how do they cope with just their cuff-partner for company?

programme.title: Stacey Solomons Renovation Rescue
programme.desc: Newlyweds Shariqa and Imani have struggled to keep their renovation project on schedule or on budget in Cambridgeshire. They dream of transforming their characterless 1970s semi-detached home into a timeless, classy home with a period feel. But with just £10,000 to add a kitchen and transform the ground floor, Stacey has her work cut out. To help Shariqa and Imani get over the line, she encourages them to take on ambitious DIY tasks, including tiling with tricky marble tiles, creating their own panelling and building flat-pack kitchen units. Stacey also brings them money-saving design ideas from colour suggestions to clever hacks using off-the-shelf items to create bespoke built-in furniture. At Pickle Cottage, Stacey takes the plunge to learn new plumbing skills as part of her project to upgrade an indoor-outdoor space shes doing up in her own home

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer try to solve the problems of first-time buyers in north London. Kirstie tries to end the house-hunting misery of Ify Okoye, who has viewed 90 properties without success. Meanwhile, Phil assists a couple looking to settle down in England after six years abroad - but discovers their ambitions may put their budget under serious strain

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug smashes into Kelly and Deacons car, causing untold damage and a war of words on what should have been a pleasant evening out. Meanwhile, Kirby teases Spence when he discovers he has an allergy to nuts. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Leah Remini

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie is invited to a company gathering, where Doug impresses the other lawyers partners with tales of his fictitious mountaineering exploits. Back at home, Arthur wrecks their bedroom and enlists Deacons help in repairing the damage before they return

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug decorates the house for Halloween against Arthurs wishes - and then regrets it when the old man has a heart attack. Meanwhile, Deacon wants to buy a comic-book character costume for his son, but is frustrated to find there arent any black superheroes. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Robert asks if he can borrow the suitcase sitting on the stairway landing, Ray explains he and Debra have been locked in a two-week battle of wills over who is responsible for putting the item away. Ray Romano and Brad Garrett star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Marie shame Ray into giving Robert a second bachelor party to make up for his disappointing first attempt

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of two. Robert and Amy prepare to tie the knot in a classic Barone family wedding ceremony - but the groom becomes suspicious when his future brother-in-law Peter tries to make peace with both him and Ray. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Brad Garrett

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Unemployed Roz is reduced to borrowing money from Frasier, but he thinks she is spending it irresponsibly. Meanwhile, rumours circulate that KACL is rehiring staff. Comedy, starring Peri Gilpin and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A friendly woman comes to Frasiers rescue when hes out Christmas shopping. He offers to return the favour, but the last thing he expects is to end up on a blind date with her daughter - while pretending to be Jewish. Meanwhile, Niles takes part in a festive pageant

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A series of good deeds backfires, embroiling Frasier in a humiliating scandal and causing him to question his faith in human nature. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer are joined by guests Greg Jenner, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Ritu Arya and Babatunde Aleshe. With music from Foy Vance

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Santas Little Helper bites Marge, the Simpsons explore the tragic past of their family dog

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart begins spying on the neighbours through Lisas telescope while recovering from an injury, and is alarmed to see something that convinces him Ned Flanders has murdered his wife

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa applies for a prestigious position in the school band, but loses out to new classmate Allison - who starts outstripping her in the popularity stakes. Upset at being relegated to second place, she resorts to sabotage in a bid to beat her rival. With the guest voice of Winona Ryder

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Patricia and Mark from East Sussex are keen to move to a country that Mark has lots of connections with. They have set their sights on Lazio in Italy, so Laura Hamilton showcases the best of what their £330,000 budget can get them in that region

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.sub-title: A VW and a Bomb Shelter
programme.desc: George explores more inspirational and ingenious amazing spaces. A father in Norfolk renovates a Volkswagen van to surprise his camping-mad kids, while a couple on the Isle of Wight turn a brick bomb shelter into a bijou bar. Master craftsman Will Hardie checks out a Russian-inspired Grayson Perry creation on the banks of the River Stour in Essex. Plus, George and Will move one step closer to completing their studio flat prototype

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: A steam engine enthusiast embarks on an ambitious coast-to-coast journey across Devon, travelling across the countys rolling hills in an 80-year-old steamroller with a top speed of eight miles per hour. Farmers on Dartmoor rear a rare breed of sheep, while at Falmouth, a group of free divers explores a spectacular underwater cave

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Toy Story (1995)
programme.desc: A jealous string-pulled cowboy dolls status as his owners favourite plaything is threatened by the arrival of a charismatic hi-tech astronaut figure, who refuses to believe he is simply a toy. However, the rivals are forced to work together when they are left stranded in the outside world. Pixar animated adventure, featuring the voices of Tom Hanks, Tim Allen, Don Rickles and Jim Varney

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Cherish Finden from Bake Off: The Professionals joins Paul Hollywood in judging the celebrity charity challenge. In a returning comedians special, reigning star baker Joe Wilkinson, Roisin Conaty, Rose Matafeo, Judi Love, Tom Davis and Jon Richardson enter the tent. They attempt breakfast pastries and matcha and yuzu biscuits, before recreating the weirdest places that theyve ever slept, in cake form

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Adventure reality series where contestants hunt each other through a forest, with one side winning if they can evade detection and the other seeking to capture them. In this opening episode, the players arrive at basecamp and meet for the very first time before being split into prey and predators. As the first hunt gets underway, an initial alliance is shattered by betrayal

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on Married at First Sight Australia, The Summit, LOL: Last One Laughing UK, Boarders, I Swear, Wheel of Fortune and Melania

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Daters who believe its never too late to find that elusive spark, including Cornwall romantics Mark and Josie, single mum Maisie and guarded Dan, and gaming duo Joe and Rebecca

programme.title: The French Dispatch (2021)
programme.desc: Wes Andersons anthology drama, following the American staff of a magazine based in a French town where the scent of revolution hangs in the air. When the editor dies, the staff including a travel writer, a cartoonist and a copy editor reminisce on the publications golden years and prepare words of wisdom for a final edition. Starring Bill Murray, Benicio Del Toro, Tilda Swinton and Jeffrey Wright

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Ramsay heads to Harpers Ferry in West Virginia, where he tries to save the Towns Inn, a historic hotel run by retired teacher and junk hoarder Karan Townsend. She has accumulated so many useless belongings that there is hardly any room to accommodate guests. Can Gordon convince her to get rid of her clutter or will the hotel continue to go down the pan?

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross presents the final of the reality challenge. With £100,000 at stake, the remaining couples take part in several challenges designed to test their friendships to the limit. Along the way, some of them are eliminated, leaving only the strongest to take on the final test - a race from Scotland to London with no phones and no money

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
