programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador puppy with an elbow disease, and believes a possible solution might involve fitted plates and stem cell injections. Elsewhere, a one-year-old cat is brought in suffering from a crushed pelvis and a broken tail after a road traffic accident, and a Russian Black Terrier with a cancerous growth is at risk of losing his leg

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: This time, Take Thats Mark Owen brings his 18-month-old Doberman, who needs treatment on a limp, into the surgery. Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. Elsewhere, a Springer Spaniel all the way from Guernsey is brought in for spinal surgery, and a Caucasian Shepherd puppy called Bear requires two hip replacements

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: In the first of a new series, Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat a giant Newfoundland puppy with a painful knee and shoulder. What appears straightforward quickly turns into a much bigger concern, and Noel and his team are challenged to invent not one but two new implants for her. Meanwhile, a rescue black Labrador called Moogi is in need of treatment for severe elbow arthritis, and a golden Labrador born with a deformed front paw is offered a bionic solution in the form of a unique, custom-made prosthesis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth are kept busy with a man vomiting blood and a patients hip pain

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Oxford, a 14-year-old boy suffers multiple fractures from a football tackle gone wrong, while in Portsmouth, paramedics meet an 81-year-old woman who has fallen from a stairlift

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Ben needs his gall bladder removing as a result of losing 11 stone, while AJ is having an operation to free the painful compressed nerves in her toes

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Connie needs a polyp taking from her womb, Carl is undergoing keyhole surgery to remove torn cartilage from his knee and Brad is having a varicose vein stripped from his leg

programme.title: The Young Midwives of Essex
programme.desc: The young midwives enjoy a night out together. Summah must think about her future on the ward, while Mollie thinks about her future off the ward

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of Americas most notorious female murderers, in a quest to discover what drives a woman to kill. He travels to Lowell Correctional Institute in Florida to talk to Rebecca Fenton, convicted for the murder of her husband Larry, who was shot dead in their home. He also looks into the details of the case, learning how for several years it seemed she was going to get away with the crime, until the involvement of the FBI helped bring her to justice

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Homestead Correctional Institution in Florida to meet Amber Wright, who, at the age of 15 conspired with a group of friends to murder her ex-boyfriend Seath Jackson, an innocent teenage boy who was beaten, shot, and knee-capped before his corpse was burnt and his remains thrown in a quarry. Later, Piers travels to meet Seaths parents in Summerfield, Florida, with their visceral anger standing in stark contrast to Ambers cool demeanour. It soon emerges that Amber was a central player in the plot to kill Seath, but will she take responsibility for her actions?

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex is hit hard by the struggles of patients going through IVF. After a previous miscarriage, Jasmine and Rumen want a second child, while Rumen is in remission for cancer

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Nearing the end of her training, Alex is allowed to assist in complex cases. She meets Holly who has a rare split womb and is attempting her sixth round of IVF with her husband

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: Too Hot to Handle - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of three. The evidence against undercover cop Stevie mounts up, suggesting she has thrown away her career for the love of a villain. Stuart finally catches up with the pair, and his suspicions are confirmed when she turns a gun on her colleague and makes her escape. With the violent Tameside Crew wreaking carnage across Sun Hill in pursuit of Towler, Stuart is suspended from duty for being too close to the case. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: Too Hot to Handle - Part Three
programme.desc: Part three of three. In a flashback sequence, Stevie reveals to Stuart how she discovered the drug deal was a set-up and why she disobeyed instructions in order to protect her boyfriend. Stuart is astonished that her erratic behaviour was designed to save his life and expose the real villain behind the Tameside Crew. As she makes plans to flee the country, the gang seems always to be two steps ahead of her - and Stevie still has to make the biggest decision of her life

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mels younger brother and his girlfriend come to stay on New Years Eve and she goes to work thinking the couple are happily celebrating in Trafalgar Square - until Sun Hill officers are called to investigate a stabbing in a club. With the troublesome youngster in the frame for the attack, apparently provoked by a fellow clubber who made advances to his companion, Mel sets out to persuade her colleagues he is innocent

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a young mother is found badly beaten in an alleyway, Sun Hill CID is drawn into her tale of debt, blackmail and a complex love life. The victim was seen arguing with a former boyfriend in a nightclub the previous day, and DNA evidence recovered from under her fingernails leads to a man who she owed a huge amount of money to. Christopher Timothy and Duncan James guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A couple return from a night away to discover their baby has been abducted from her bed. The childs nanny - the last person to see her - gives a garbled account of events, covering for her boyfriend who had visited on the night in question. But as Tony gently interrogates the parents, another suspect emerges

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Its Halloween and Matron Hazera is having a staffing nightmare, Matron Steph meets an abusive patient in police custody, and Amy helps deliver premature identical twins

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show

Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex has now been a trainee fertility assistant for two months and meets two couples desperately trying to start their families

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies

programme.title: My Family

programme.title: My Family

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Roger and Abi announce they have set a date for their wedding, which sets Susan thinking about what a disappointment her own big day was. programme.title: My Family

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Michael succeeds in getting the Harpers accepted to appear on The Weakest Link, but some searching questions give the familys dirty washing a good airing on national TV - not least that Susan was once married to a wrestler called Troy the Punisher. John Burgess and Rosemary Leach guest star, with Anne Robinson as herself

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador puppy with an elbow disease, and believes a possible solution might involve fitted plates and stem cell injections. Elsewhere, a one-year-old cat is brought in suffering from a crushed pelvis and a broken tail after a road traffic accident, and a Russian Black Terrier with a cancerous growth is at risk of losing his leg

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: This time, Take Thats Mark Owen brings his 18-month-old Doberman, who needs treatment on a limp, into the surgery. Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. Elsewhere, a Springer Spaniel all the way from Guernsey is brought in for spinal surgery, and a Caucasian Shepherd puppy called Bear requires two hip replacements

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: In the first of a new series, Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat a giant Newfoundland puppy with a painful knee and shoulder. What appears straightforward quickly turns into a much bigger concern, and Noel and his team are challenged to invent not one but two new implants for her. Meanwhile, a rescue black Labrador called Moogi is in need of treatment for severe elbow arthritis, and a golden Labrador born with a deformed front paw is offered a bionic solution in the form of a unique, custom-made prosthesis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth are kept busy with a man vomiting blood and a patients hip pain

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Oxford, a 14-year-old boy suffers multiple fractures from a football tackle gone wrong, while in Portsmouth, paramedics meet an 81-year-old woman who has fallen from a stairlift

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Ben needs his gall bladder removing as a result of losing 11 stone, while AJ is having an operation to free the painful compressed nerves in her toes

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Connie needs a polyp taking from her womb, Carl is undergoing keyhole surgery to remove torn cartilage from his knee and Brad is having a varicose vein stripped from his leg

programme.title: The Young Midwives of Essex
programme.desc: The young midwives enjoy a night out together. Summah must think about her future on the ward, while Mollie thinks about her future off the ward

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of Americas most notorious female murderers, in a quest to discover what drives a woman to kill. He travels to Lowell Correctional Institute in Florida to talk to Rebecca Fenton, convicted for the murder of her husband Larry, who was shot dead in their home. He also looks into the details of the case, learning how for several years it seemed she was going to get away with the crime, until the involvement of the FBI helped bring her to justice

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Homestead Correctional Institution in Florida to meet Amber Wright, who, at the age of 15 conspired with a group of friends to murder her ex-boyfriend Seath Jackson, an innocent teenage boy who was beaten, shot, and knee-capped before his corpse was burnt and his remains thrown in a quarry. Later, Piers travels to meet Seaths parents in Summerfield, Florida, with their visceral anger standing in stark contrast to Ambers cool demeanour. It soon emerges that Amber was a central player in the plot to kill Seath, but will she take responsibility for her actions?

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex is hit hard by the struggles of patients going through IVF. After a previous miscarriage, Jasmine and Rumen want a second child, while Rumen is in remission for cancer

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on train journeys through Edwardian Britain, exploring the grand estates of Norfolk and a bivouac on Brownsea Island on the way. He also learns the ropes aboard an Edwardian wherry on the Norfolk Broads

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Letchworth Garden City to Herne Hill
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores an Edwardian utopia in Letchworth, where he meets a fourth generation citizen who introduces him to the towns community spirit. He then heads towards London, and discovers a favoured haunt of King Edward VII, before sampling the monarchs favourite tipple, the Kings Ginger

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Croydon, where he hears the story of a mixed-race composer who was once celebrated but is now forgotten. At Three Bridges station, he is delighted to find a recently built depot and its fleet of new Class 700 trains - and is given the great honour of washing one down. In Lewes, he makes a beeline for Charleston, the home of artists Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant, and in Shoreham-by-Sea, Michael discovers how magnificent Edwardians in flying machines took off from the oldest licensed airport in the country

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Chichester, West Sussex, where he encounters an Edwardian motoring duo patronised by King Edward VII. After a grand tour of their factory, he is treated to some rest and relaxation in one of their finest vehicles. Arriving in the resort of Southsea, Hampshire, Michael heads for the Kings Theatre, where he treads the boards in a community production of Lads in the Village. Crossing the Solent, the presenter boards the Island Line to travel along the pier in a 1930s London Underground carriage, then hops on to the Isle of Wight heritage line bound for Wootton

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.desc: A unique visit behind the scenes with the small team in Dartford who hand-build the iconic open-top, two-seater Caterham 7 sports car

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at how Liverpool supercar manufacturer Briggs Automotive Company make their £270k BAC Mono R

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.desc: We follow the 35-hour build of a new Defender, where over 3300 individual parts and 70 years of innovation come together to create a vehicle for any terrain

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car

programme.desc: Resistance leader Michelle blackmails Von Strohm and Gruber for 10 million francs, after revealing she has a photograph of the pair holding the Fallen Madonna, while Herr Flick gives Helga a truth drug to discover the location of the painting

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve uncovers extraordinary stories and experiences the diverse collection of cultures located in the southern tropics. The first leg of his journey sees the globe-trotting presenter cross the sweeping vistas of Namibia and Botswana, where he witnesses the vestiges of German colonisation in Swakopmund and searches for the Bushmen of the Kalahari desert

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve continues his quest to trace the southern tropic, travelling from South Africa to Madagascar. In Zimbabwe, the presenter comes face to face with the harsh realities of the refugee crisis, and witnesses a unique method of land-mine disposal in Mozambique. Heading to the Madagascan islands, he discovers an unusual local delicacy, and visits a town where the poor make a living by digging for sapphires

programme.title: Inside Bentley: A Great British Motorcar
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at the car companys plant as work gets underway on a range of 4x4s, from the factory floor to the worlds most exclusive car showroom

programme.desc: A collection of Austin Mini Metros arrive for auction. A baby Fiat 850 coupe also goes under the hammer and a haulier offloads part of his vintage commercial vehicle collection

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has three scooters to collect, while also finding room for a Daimler and a teardrop caravan

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks on his way to Blackpool to pick up what will be the highest priced item at the next auction. But, the 1970 Aston Martin DBS needs a tow to the local garage

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of present scouts, the presenter learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys - and girls - today. In the New Forest, Michael searches out some unsung heroes of the First World War - the ponies who became war horses - and hears their story from a commoner who runs a stable and riding school. Then in Bournemouth, the one-time MP investigates a very British institution - the beach hut

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall, travelling from Whitland, Carmarthenshire, to Swansea

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Pontyclun to Ebbw Vale in Wales. Along the way, he visits the Royal Mint in Llantrisant and Cardiff Arms Park, scene of the first Welsh victory over the All Blacks back in 1905. In Cardiff Bay, Michael discovers the departure point for one of the Edwardian eras most famous expeditions - Scotts ill-fated voyage to the Antarctic

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Newport, Gwent, where he learns about the heroism of a young boy who was awarded the Albert Medal, before visiting a cinema dating from 1912 in Clevedon

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: The actor begins the next part of his journey on Mundoo Island, which has provided shelter and a way of life for five generations of one farming family. Martin Clunes joins Colin and Sally Grundy for a day of cattle mustering. He also explores Phillip Island, one of Australias favourite playgrounds, then heads south to King Island, in the blustery Bass Strait between Tasmania and mainland Australia. Martin completes his odyssey with a visit to see one of the regions most iconic creatures, the Tasmanian Devil

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of America
programme.desc: The actor travels around the coast of the United States to discover what life is like on the surrounding islands. He sets off on a 10,000-mile journey from the west to the east, to explore the vast swathe of islands that are scattered beyond Americas shores. The first port of call on his journey is Hawaii, and his exploration looks beyond its image as a tourist paradise, crammed with sun-kissed beaches and world-class surfing, and into the havoc wreaked by advancing lava fields and smoking craters. Leaving Hawaii, Martin flies 2,500 miles north to the islands of Alaska, where the contrast couldnt be more extreme, from tropical rain forests to snow-fringed glaciers, and from crowded beaches to some of Americas most sparsely populated territories

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Two years after Cuba announced radical economic reforms, broadcaster Simon Reeve heads to the Caribbean island to look at the effects the liberalisation has had. He meets Cubans whose lives are being transformed - from the owners of fledgling businesses to the newly rich estate agents selling properties valued at up to £750,000 - and asks if this new openness could lead to more political freedom. He also explores whether Cuba will be able to maintain the positive aspects of its long isolation under socialism - such as low crime, high-quality education and one of the best healthcare systems in the world - while embracing a form of capitalism. Part of the This World strand

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer returns with a series that takes him around the world to areas that cannot be fully appreciated without considering the vast influence of their rivers, and hes going to explain how those sacred rivers dont just simply flow through countries - they define them. Simon begins at the source of the worlds longest river, the Nile, and follows it out to sea, taking him from the Ethiopian highlands, through the desert of Sudan and then Egypt, right to the Mediterranean Sea. He also explains how the Nile has forged some of our earliest civilisations and influenced religions. However, the stability of the region looks uncertain, given that populations along its banks are growing, and Nile countries are demanding greater shares of the sacred waters

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on train journeys through Edwardian Britain, exploring the grand estates of Norfolk and a bivouac on Brownsea Island on the way. He also learns the ropes aboard an Edwardian wherry on the Norfolk Broads

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Letchworth Garden City to Herne Hill
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores an Edwardian utopia in Letchworth, where he meets a fourth generation citizen who introduces him to the towns community spirit. He then heads towards London, and discovers a favoured haunt of King Edward VII, before sampling the monarchs favourite tipple, the Kings Ginger

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Croydon, where he hears the story of a mixed-race composer who was once celebrated but is now forgotten. At Three Bridges station, he is delighted to find a recently built depot and its fleet of new Class 700 trains - and is given the great honour of washing one down. In Lewes, he makes a beeline for Charleston, the home of artists Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant, and in Shoreham-by-Sea, Michael discovers how magnificent Edwardians in flying machines took off from the oldest licensed airport in the country

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Chichester, West Sussex, where he encounters an Edwardian motoring duo patronised by King Edward VII. After a grand tour of their factory, he is treated to some rest and relaxation in one of their finest vehicles. Arriving in the resort of Southsea, Hampshire, Michael heads for the Kings Theatre, where he treads the boards in a community production of Lads in the Village. Crossing the Solent, the presenter boards the Island Line to travel along the pier in a 1930s London Underground carriage, then hops on to the Isle of Wight heritage line bound for Wootton

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.desc: A unique visit behind the scenes with the small team in Dartford who hand-build the iconic open-top, two-seater Caterham 7 sports car

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at how Liverpool supercar manufacturer Briggs Automotive Company make their £270k BAC Mono R

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.desc: We follow the 35-hour build of a new Defender, where over 3300 individual parts and 70 years of innovation come together to create a vehicle for any terrain

programme.title: How to Build a Motor Car
programme.desc: Factory 56 opens its doors as we follow the ultimate configuration of a £120,000 Mercedes-Benz S-Class, from the chassis build to the main assembly floor

programme.title: Allo Allo!

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: Resistance leader Michelle blackmails Von Strohm and Gruber for 10 million francs, after revealing she has a photograph of the pair holding the Fallen Madonna, while Herr Flick gives Helga a truth drug to discover the location of the painting

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: Its Renes birthday and surprises include a car from Edith, a wink from Hitler and a bomb from the Resistance. The celebration concludes with a bang

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom Chinea tries out water dowsing so he can fill his new pond. He and Sam Lovegrove fix some church bells and then put a surf lifesaving clubs quad bike back on the beach

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve uncovers extraordinary stories and experiences the diverse collection of cultures located in the southern tropics. The first leg of his journey sees the globe-trotting presenter cross the sweeping vistas of Namibia and Botswana, where he witnesses the vestiges of German colonisation in Swakopmund and searches for the Bushmen of the Kalahari desert

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve continues his quest to trace the southern tropic, travelling from South Africa to Madagascar. In Zimbabwe, the presenter comes face to face with the harsh realities of the refugee crisis, and witnesses a unique method of land-mine disposal in Mozambique. Heading to the Madagascan islands, he discovers an unusual local delicacy, and visits a town where the poor make a living by digging for sapphires

programme.title: Inside Bentley: A Great British Motorcar
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at the car companys plant as work gets underway on a range of 4x4s, from the factory floor to the worlds most exclusive car showroom

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of present scouts, the presenter learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys - and girls - today. In the New Forest, Michael searches out some unsung heroes of the First World War - the ponies who became war horses - and hears their story from a commoner who runs a stable and riding school. Then in Bournemouth, the one-time MP investigates a very British institution - the beach hut

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall, travelling from Whitland, Carmarthenshire, to Swansea

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Pontyclun to Ebbw Vale in Wales. Along the way, he visits the Royal Mint in Llantrisant and Cardiff Arms Park, scene of the first Welsh victory over the All Blacks back in 1905. In Cardiff Bay, Michael discovers the departure point for one of the Edwardian eras most famous expeditions - Scotts ill-fated voyage to the Antarctic

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Newport, Gwent, where he learns about the heroism of a young boy who was awarded the Albert Medal, before visiting a cinema dating from 1912 in Clevedon

programme.desc: Robbie navigates the wintery Grand Union Canal while preparing for Christmas, and narrowly avoids flooding his boat in a tricky staircase lock

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: The actor begins the next part of his journey on Mundoo Island, which has provided shelter and a way of life for five generations of one farming family. Martin Clunes joins Colin and Sally Grundy for a day of cattle mustering. He also explores Phillip Island, one of Australias favourite playgrounds, then heads south to King Island, in the blustery Bass Strait between Tasmania and mainland Australia. Martin completes his odyssey with a visit to see one of the regions most iconic creatures, the Tasmanian Devil

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of America
programme.desc: The actor travels around the coast of the United States to discover what life is like on the surrounding islands. He sets off on a 10,000-mile journey from the west to the east, to explore the vast swathe of islands that are scattered beyond Americas shores. The first port of call on his journey is Hawaii, and his exploration looks beyond its image as a tourist paradise, crammed with sun-kissed beaches and world-class surfing, and into the havoc wreaked by advancing lava fields and smoking craters. Leaving Hawaii, Martin flies 2,500 miles north to the islands of Alaska, where the contrast couldnt be more extreme, from tropical rain forests to snow-fringed glaciers, and from crowded beaches to some of Americas most sparsely populated territories

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Two years after Cuba announced radical economic reforms, broadcaster Simon Reeve heads to the Caribbean island to look at the effects the liberalisation has had. He meets Cubans whose lives are being transformed - from the owners of fledgling businesses to the newly rich estate agents selling properties valued at up to £750,000 - and asks if this new openness could lead to more political freedom. He also explores whether Cuba will be able to maintain the positive aspects of its long isolation under socialism - such as low crime, high-quality education and one of the best healthcare systems in the world - while embracing a form of capitalism. Part of the This World strand

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer returns with a series that takes him around the world to areas that cannot be fully appreciated without considering the vast influence of their rivers, and hes going to explain how those sacred rivers dont just simply flow through countries - they define them. Simon begins at the source of the worlds longest river, the Nile, and follows it out to sea, taking him from the Ethiopian highlands, through the desert of Sudan and then Egypt, right to the Mediterranean Sea. He also explains how the Nile has forged some of our earliest civilisations and influenced religions. However, the stability of the region looks uncertain, given that populations along its banks are growing, and Nile countries are demanding greater shares of the sacred waters

programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Paul OGradys Animal Orphans
programme.desc: In his final week in Africa, the comedian and presenter visits Bambelela, a rehabilitation centre for orphaned vervet monkeys, where he looks after 10-month-old Toledo for the day. Returning to Moholoholo Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre, Paul discovers what its like to be a volunteer, from the daily walk through the Bush, to cleaning the enclosures and encountering the smelliest animal at the facility, a honey badger called Julius. After that, he heads to Hoedspruit Endangered Species Centre to meet Little G, an orphaned rhino who has been given a permanent companion, a sheep named Lamby

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: A French bulldog arrives at the vets for specialist spinal surgery to help him walk properly. Staff in the large animal department have to decide if a wild seals eye is able to be saved, and nurse Clodagh and vet Myles have to look long and hard to find a magnet in a parrots gizzard

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A teenage boy is brought into the Royal having fallen from a cliff, but it soon emerges that the incident was a suicide attempt as evidence of extreme depression comes to the surface. Dr Cheriton contacts the youngsters psychiatrist, but is taken aback when electroshock therapy is suggested as a form of treatment, so ventures to the family home to recommend an alternative - only for tragedy to strike. Meanwhile, a hypochondriac causes chaos at the hospital when she insists her dog be treated following a snake bite, and a heart-to-heart talk between Drs Weatherill and Ormerod leads to a passionate kiss. Honor Blackman guest stars

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A family hunting expedition ends in a youngsters tragic death, and Ken clashes with Harper over the hospital laundry. programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The five-time Paralympian recalls her life as an elite swimmer and her journey to gold in Rio 2016

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, bricklayer Alex, support worker Kevin and plasterer Zhane are all hoping to win a date with Shannon, a self-proclaimed modern-day Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: Deirdre OKane provides the voiceover as a selection of telly addicts pass comment on the weeks programmes from the comfort of their living rooms. From December 2016

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a charity edition of the game show, with Ben Bailey Smith, Claire Richards and Lisa Riley all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi returns to Rosehill House, a 30-bedroom hotel in Burnley. When Alex first arrived two and a half years ago, she met a dad with a deadline and a daughter in despair. With the hotel badly underperforming, John was threatening to sell it and turn it into flats, but daughter Emma was unable to let it go. Have they managed to turn the business around?

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Staff at Hotel Benidorm Plaza are trying to keep their cool as the reception air conditioning has a temporary malfunction, while guest experience manager Rosanna sweeps Holly and Dan off to one of Benidorms best-kept secrets. Owner Fede and his wife are turning 40 and have challenged hotel manager Angel to throw them a party at the hotel for the great and the good of Benidorm.

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner have advice on baking, including a classic coffee and walnut cake, a traybake featuring fish, potatoes and croutons, and strawberry shortcake crunch

programme.title: Champions: Full Gallop
programme.desc: Documentary following the British horse-racing jump season, beginning as it bursts into action at Cheltenhams November meeting. Rival jockeys Harry Cobden, Nico de Boinville, Sean Bowen and Harry Skelton begin a battle in the all-new David Power Jockey Cup, a league format showdown with £500,000 in prize money up for grabs, with emotions running high as rising star Freddie Gingell steps into the spotlight

programme.title: The Floor
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the grand finale of the game show, with the remaining nine contestants facing one last series of high-stakes duels. Two players will compete in a best-of-three final, but only one of them will conquer the Floor and take home the £50,000 jackpot

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show which takes place in public locations around the UK. He invites passers-by in Newcastle to take part in a series of unusual challenges, with a prize of £1,000 up for grabs

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the exciting and fast-paced quiz where each episode sees six hopefuls compete for £1 million - however, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Cherry Healey, Dallas Campbell, Ria Hebden and Robert Peston pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: The daily reality for rookie nurses - especially in AandE - as they deal with life-or-death situations and everything in between. Twenty-two-year-old Mikey is part of the trauma team tasked with helping a teenage rugby player brought in by ambulance with severe concussion in Carmarthen. In Aberystwyth, 24-year-old nurse is Leah is unphased when a patient arrives having suffered a freak lawnmower accident which led to an amputation. Amid medical dramas on the wards, theres a big moment for healthcare assistant Caitlin who, after years of studying, is on her way to collect her new nurse uniform, and is excited to join her colleagues in her blues

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: The auditions make their way to Birminghams Hippodrome Theatre, where guest judge Stacey Solomon - who knows a thing or two about auditioning in front of a live audience, after coming third on The X Factor in 2009 - joins the panel alongside KSI, Alesha Dixon and Amanda Holden

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.sub-title: David James, Lucrezia Millarini, Myles Stephenson and Joe Sugg
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as David James, Lucrezia Millarini, Myles Stephenson and Joe Suggn pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: The presenter is joined by comedian and award-winning podcaster Joanne McNally, who speaks to Claudia about her record-breaking stand-up tour, Pinotphile. Bafta-nominated comedian and actor Guz Khan speaks about his new Amazon Prime series Bait, which he stars in alongside Riz Ahmed. Former One Direction star and singer-songwriter Niall Horan joins Claudia to share how he went from global boy band success to solo chart-topping artist and talk about his new album, featuring his latest single Dinner Party

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Andrew and Fergie: An Unusual Relationship
programme.desc: Documentary focused on the life of King Charles IIIs troubled younger brother, who has publicly fallen from grace. His former wife is the outspoken Duchess Sarah Ferguson, who apparently cant avoid trouble in the eyes of the media. Together they make up the most controversial duo in the British royal family

programme.title: Coma
programme.desc: Simon takes matters into his own hands, threatening his old boss Jimmy to fast-track his redundancy payment - otherwise he will reveal that Jimmy got rid of him as part of a personal vendetta. Meanwhile, DS Evans follows up a call Simon made the night before the attack, reporting a threatening local youth

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Journey to the Edge of the World
programme.desc: The comedian boards a Russian cruise ship to make an eight-day trip through the Northwest Passage and visit the sites where explorers have lived and died. Stopping off on Beechey Island, where Victorian naval officer John Franklin was stranded for two years, Billy moves on to Gjoa Haven, a settlement on King William Island where Roald Amundsen stayed 50 years after Franklins expedition. He then heads south to Dawson City, the site of a gold rush in the late 19th century

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: With the Benidorm Plaza full to bursting, and housekeeping pushed to their limits, the clock is ticking as bride-to-be Hannah and her hen party are keen to check into their rooms to get the party started. Meanwhile, restaurant manager Pepe is challenged by new hotel manager Angel to revamp his weekly Gala Dinner

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner have recipes for holiday favourites, including indulgent French toast served two ways, as well as Lisas Spanish-style stew served with Johns aioli. Plus, seasoned tofu steaks accompanied by a rainbow salad and homemade sweet chilli sauce, and finally peaches and cream pies and a roasted peach daiquiri for dessert

programme.title: Champions: Full Gallop
programme.desc: It is Boxing Day at Kempton and the stage is set for a Christmas showdown like no other. A sold-out crowd roars with anticipation as the unbeaten Constitution Hill and brilliant Irish mare Lossiemouth prepare to face off in the Christmas Hurdle. The build-up has been intense - as two of the best jockeys in the saddle also go head-to-head, who will triumph?

programme.title: For the Love of Dogs with Alison Hammond
programme.desc: Alison meets the most unlikely stray to be wandering the London streets in the form of a rare Romanian mioritic shepherd who has a fear of stairs and doorways. She also helps a tiny Bichon control its bark, and there is organised chaos when a van with seven Belgian shepherd cross puppies arrives from the RSPCA

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: The daily reality for rookie nurses - especially in AandE - as they deal with life-or-death situations and everything in between. programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Prank show in which Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry work together to deceive unsuspecting members of the public by making them believe they are participating in new ITV shows. The first set of unsuspecting contestants think they are taking part in business competition The Applicant, hosted by Dragons Den star Sara Davies - who is in on the joke - while Jo Brand joins in the fun from Mission HQ as the rebellious guest prankster

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz in which six hopefuls compete for £1 million, with only one player at a time able to take a place in the Hot Seat and answer multiple-choice questions to work the way up the Money Ladder. Each question is against the clock and if a player gets one wrong they are eliminated, the Top Prize drops down, and the next player takes their place

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Richard Bacon, Daisy Arwen Campbell, Neil Jones and Gary Wilmot pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Tati and her team don hazmat gear to clean a filthy home overrun by pets. Window cleaners battle it out for employee of the month and Dublin Citys night crew scrub the streets

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Shergar: The Racehorse and the IRA
programme.desc: Set against the backdrop of IRA activity, this documentary investigates the extraordinary 1983 kidnapping of Shergar, a Derby-winning racehorse and global icon

programme.title: Tour de Fred
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix takes to the streets and roads of Cork City, testing his stamina with a cycle challenge up St Patricks Hill, one of the citys most famous climbs. He samples Corks famous stout at Murphys Brewery, trains with a womens GAA team, discovers Corks past as the worlds butter capital, tries ceili dancing and cycles to Blarney Castle

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Comedy game show in which celebrities team up with family members, with the parents choosing which questions their children will answer and gambling on how many they can get right. The first edition features Match of the Day presenter Gabby Logan and her daughter Lois, reality TV star Pete Wicks and his mum Tracey and journalist Krishnan Guru-Murthy and his daughter Jasmine

programme.title: Betrayal
programme.desc: John is still reeling after his discovery that a proxy attack is possibly being planned on UK soil to target a prominent Iranian dissident, but a visit to an informants widow throws up contradictory evidence. John becomes convinced that there is a cover-up that permeates to the highest level. Meanwhile, Johns refusal to accept voluntary redundancy means his relationship with Claire is on thin ice, and a visit from his in-laws only stokes tensions. Thriller, starring Shaun Evans and Romola Garai

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.desc: Tim returns home on bail and talks to Dani about their deteriorating marriage. He also voices his fears of going to prison while his wife resolves to clear his name. Jimmy tries to smooth tension with Matt, who decides to leave and stay with his mum

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny falls victim to conmen who have devised an elaborate loan and savings racket to fleece unwitting villagers. Meanwhile, Vernon, Bernie and David put their literary prowess to the test by entering a poetry competition run by the Ashfordly Gazette. Rural drama, starring Sarah Tansey and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamys fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. Elsewhere, Vernon decides to venture into the BandB market - but his cooking skills leave a lot to be desired. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps embarks on a mission to make Aidensfield sparkle with his own brand of cleaning products. Drama, starring Aislin McGuckin and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Cheap Flat
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navys top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bear makes a huge revelation, while Graham works hard to keep Joe on side and Jacob is struggling at work

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy and Dylan make a big confession, while the Dingles are suspicious of Joe Tate and Pollard plays detective

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy and Dylans nightmare deepens, while Laurel is asked for a favour and Kim is suspicious of Joe

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dylan is struggling as his impending court date approaches, while Graham confronts Joe, and Mary gives Pollard some advice

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Isaac is upset that his dad has cancer, while Cain is preoccupied in his search for farmland and Pollard tests Kerrys loyalty

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian returns to Battersea Dogs and Cats Home in London, meeting Spencer the stray beagle, who is consigned to quarantine with a contagious virus and petrified of humans. Paul also meets Lydia, the Hungarian Puli, a heavily dreadlocked rare pedigree, who is struggling in the hot summer heat, and Tintin the tearaway Yorkshire terrier who he takes for a trip to the hairdressers

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Oxford plays host to a veteran film star, in town to visit a local cinema. However, a mysterious death casts a sinister pall over the occasion, and offers the newly promoted DS Endeavour Morse a fresh case to pursue. Meanwhile, a family visit puts DCI Thursdays hospitality to the test. Inspector Morse prequel, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the stories of three men brought up in the care system who are trying to reconnect with the families that were taken from them. Murray Phillips was adopted from a childrens home as a young boy with his older brother Ricky, who was sent back to the childrens home when he was 12, leaving nine-year-old Murray completely devastated and confused. Eighty-seven-year-old Mark Chesterfield spent a large part of his childhood at a foundling hospital and has been trying to reclaim his identity ever since he found out at the age of 74 that he wasnt an orphan, as hed always believed, but had a mother whod wanted to be part of his life. Having traced her side of the family, he came to Long Lost Family to find out about his fathers side

programme.title: Titanic
programme.desc: The epic drama reaches its conclusion. The ship hits the iceberg and panic takes hold. As the women and children board the lifeboats, Georgiana and Harry savour the few precious moments they have together, Barnes gives Watson a parting gift that will change her life for ever, and Louisa makes a surprising promise to Hugh. Below decks, Paolo tries desperately to save his brother and Jim searches for his daughter. As the liner is dragged underwater, the next few hours will decide who will live. And who will not. Linus Roache, Geraldine Somerville, Perdita Weeks, Lee Ross and Lyndsey Marshal star

programme.title: The Secret Life of Our Pets
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the relationships people have with their pets. The first episode examines the extraordinary science behind interspecies communications, using science and technology to reveal the unique ways animals have of expressing themselves, from the dog communicating with his owner using a system of buttons, to the parrots fooling Alexa into adding their favourite treats to the weekly shop

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: My Hotel Nightmare
programme.desc: There has been plenty of progress around the hotel, including the bedrooms in the sixties block and the new outside courtyard. Interior designer Olga can start to bring her design vision together, and even afford a little time away to shop for antiques. This leaves Alex to oversee the parts of the hotel that are of more concern

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: Rising music star Gaia Lafayette announces to her devoted fans that she and her band are headed to Los Angeles, but a shocking attack leads Grace and Branson to believe someone may be trying to harm her. With a perpetrator on the loose, Roy must deduce who of Gaias fanbase may be concealing sinister intentions. Meanwhile, a grisly discovery on a remote Sussex pig farm puts the detective under serious pressure, forcing him to split his team in order to investigate two impossible lines of enquiry. Crime drama, starring John Simm

programme.title: Unstable (2009)
programme.desc: A woman recovering from an abusive relationship falls for a seemingly trustworthy man and marries him. However, a series of strange events leaves her unable to tell if her new husband is secretly playing mind games or if her previous trauma is causing her to go insane. Thriller, starring Shiri Appleby and David Alpay

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, looking at the events leading up to crimes, going through the police investigations and summing up the trials

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve comes to the aid of an amnesiac struggling to piece together his identity, Jenny tells Dennis she is tired of playing second fiddle to his career, and Bernie and David step in when it appears Vernons money-making schemes are getting the better of him. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Ian East, Peter Benson, David Lonsdale and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Alf Ventress mounts a large-scale surveillance operation in an effort to track down the wool thief responsible for de-fleecing sheep in Aidensfield. Bernie finds himself in hot water when an estranged son arrives to bury his father - who is already six feet under. Drama, starring James Carlton and Peter Benson

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A serious assault leads the police to foil an attempt to steal antiques from Ashfordly Hall, and Vernon goes all-out to persuade a wealthy woman to make a charitable contribution toward his donkey sanctuary - but the discovery that there is already one in the area forces him to change his plans. Meanwhile, Merton devises a solution to Ventresss retirement worries. Rosemary Leach guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UKs prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Western Star
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sam falls unconscious in the street, Kit encounters a witness to his assault on Mal, and Christina orders the undertakers to cheer up

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne persuades Sam to share his troubles, Kit is forced to question his own victim, Alya fails to match Adams enthusiasm, and Todd spots Theo lunching with another man

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sam is supported by his family while Megan faces the music, Adam and Alya strike a deal with Rich, and Todd agrees with Theo that its time to move on

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Driscolls make a shocking discovery as Megans lie is exposed, Adams professional reputation faces ruin, and a jealous Theo keeps an eye on Todds blind date

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel seeks answers from Megan about the future of their relationship, and Theo hatches a new plan, announcing that hes been offered a job in Belfast before spiking an oblivious Todds drink

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian meets three dogs in need of companionship. First up is excitable 10-month-old mongrel Blake, who loves playing with his toys - but when Paul suspects his eyesight is not as it should be and the vets confirm his fears, the presenter is given the challenge of finding the canine a suitable friend to act as his very own guide dog. Paul also meets Jacko the Jack Russell, who has been down in the dumps ever since arriving at Battersea, and 14-week-old Saluki Forest, who is in quarantine due to a stomach bug. Paul tries to come up with a way of keeping the puppy entertained - but with no physical contact allowed, that proves easier said than done

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse suspects a connection between a womans disappearance and the unsolved murder of a teenager several years previously. However, the discovery of a body and some unusual elements to the crime scene suggest another possibility. Thursday investigates a lorry hijack which he believes could be the work of a local gangster, and Fancy takes on an informant who is subsequently murdered

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2016

programme.title: The Mayfair Hotel Megabuild
programme.desc: Just over halfway through the seven-year renovation project the team adopts unusual measures to make way for the next stage of the audacious build - a four-storey roof extension. Meanwhile, after the challenges of groundwater and an underground river have been overcome, the digging out of the basement reaches its fifth and deepest level

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave and Si cross the state of Arizona in search of the Wild West and make a small detour from Route 66, starting in Monument Valley, to learn more about this striking place. Theyre invited by a local Navajo police officer to eat with his family at a traditional Navajo barbecue, where a sheep is butchered, grilled and eaten alongside some traditional fry bread. Still riding in the Navajo Nation, the bikers stop at Tsaile College to learn more about rodeo and cook a lamb stew with corn dumplings in homage of the Navajo people they met

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.sub-title: Long Lost Family Special: The Unknown Soldiers
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell join forces with investigators from the MODs Joint Casualty and Compassionate Centre to try to trace the families of nine First World War soldiers whose bodies were found together in a trench by roadworkers in Beselare, Belgium

programme.title: Shetland
programme.desc: With the main suspect for the murder of Ellen Quinn in custody, Tosh orders the team to find enough evidence to charge them. Calder struggles with Jeans revelation about her father. The team find the culprit for the recent animal killings

programme.title: Michael Palin in Nigeria
programme.desc: In the last leg of his journey, Michael heads to the city of Benin, where he learns about the 1897 Raid on Benin, viewing a commemorative statue and visiting Igun Street to see bronze statues being made. Onboard a boat on the River Ethiope, he meets Nigerian filmmaker Chuko Esiri. They discuss Nigerias wildlife and the inspiration that Chuko gets from this location

programme.title: Chicago Fire
programme.desc: Severide fights the sexual harassment charges and turns to an unlikely ally, while things start to look up for Casey. Elsewhere, a touch-and-go rescue mission at a collapsed building helps Mills and Dawson realise what - and who - they really want

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Adam and Rachel try to spice up their sex life by indulging their deepest darkest fantasies, while David frets over his own shortcomings between the sheets and Pete discovers the downside of fatherhood. Comedy-drama, starring Helen Baxendale, James Nesbitt, John Thomson, Fay Ripley, Robert Bathurst and Hermione Norris

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, looking at the events leading up to crimes, going through the police investigations and summing up the trials

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Suspicion falls on Ginas new cellar man Len Carter when a valuable pendant is stolen from Emily Forster, a guest at the Aidensfield Arms. A search into the barmans past reveals a history of crime, but Emily appears more disturbed by the presence of pub comedian Tommy Tanner. Meanwhile, Peggy secures a good deal on a new vehicle and Geoffs need for speed gets the better of him

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Martha smudge the mayors mansion for her housewarming gala. Abigail and Donovan reunite and put up a final fight against the Merriwick-Davenport curse

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The incredible efforts to ensure that the hastily constructed temporary fix around the damaged reactor at Chernobyl becomes a permanent solution, known as the New Safe Confinement

programme.title: Judi Loves Culinary Cruise
programme.desc: The presenter stops at the archaeological site of Delphi in Greece, where she visits its famous ruins, meets the inhabitants of a bee farm, and tastes their honey. Judi also visits a family run bakery, where she makes the famous kaitafi dessert with owner Sophocles, has a party with pastry, and cooks up a dish of honey and rum peaches with mini doughnuts

programme.title: Love Island USA Aftersun
programme.desc: The weekly studio show that recaps and delivers all the romantic twists, turns and favourite moments from the previous weeks five nights of the main series.

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from the talk show. Among the guests featured are Madonna, Dave Bautista, Mel Giedroyc, Peter Kay, Spice Girls, Harrison Ford, Glenn Close, Mo Gilligan, Keira Knightley and Bear Grylls

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Among the guests featured are Gordon Ramsay, Britney Spears, Idris Elba, Lena Dunham, Stormzy, Joanna Lumley, Cristiano Ronaldo, Aisling Bea and Jack Black

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Magazine Show
programme.desc: A review of the latest matches in Europes premier club competition, plus a look ahead to forthcoming fixtures

programme.title: Live World Athletics Indoor Championships
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day two at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The mens heptathlon continues with the 60m hurdles and pole vault, while the heats of the mens 60m hurdles also take place, before the session concludes with the mens and womens 800m semi-finals. The other notable event is the final of the mixed 4x400m relay, which is making its debut at this championship

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Live: Formula E
programme.desc: The Madrid ePrix (Start-time 2.05pm). Coverage of the sixth round of the season, which takes place at Circuito del Jarama

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Well-off Tony and Sarah Page from West Yorkshire leave their eight-bedroom house behind to trade places with the Hollands, who live in a three-bedroom council house in a Hartlepool, and are in the countrys poorest 10 per cent. They swap homes, budgets and lives for seven days as they see what it is like at the other end of the economic spectrum

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Megan cleans Dublins streets the morning after the night before, Tati makes a surprise inspection to see if standards are being met and John Hurt deploys his drain robots

programme.title: Live World Athletics Indoor Championships
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day two at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The mens heptathlon concludes with the 1,000m, which last year saw Norways Sander Skotheim claim the gold medal with a narrow victory over Johannes Erm, despite the Estonians victory in this discipline, as he had to settle for silver. Elsewhere, gold medals will also be awarded in eight disciplines, including the mens and womens 400m and 3,000m, the mens 60m hurdles, and womens 60m

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including an Essex pawnbroker who has put off his wedding three times because he has lost confidence in his looks and a biker from Newport Pagnell, who rocks up wanting a funked-up feminine look. The experts also get their hands on a stay-at-home mum who wants her fashion mojo back after battling a brain condition and a lifelong military man who would like a smart casual look perfect for a single gentleman in his sixties. Plus, advice for viewers on what jeans work best for different body shapes

programme.title: Side Hustlers
programme.desc: Investors Emma Grede and Ashley Graham help tenacious female entrepreneurs transform their side hustles into investment-worthy into full-time businesses - all in just 40 days

programme.desc: Deirdre OKane provides the voiceover as a selection of telly addicts pass comment on the weeks programmes from the comfort of their living rooms. From December 2016

programme.title: Love Island USA
programme.desc: The American version of the relationship-based reality show, in which singletons compete to pair off while living in a villa

programme.title: Iain Stirlings CelebAbility
programme.desc: Scarlett Moffat leads a group of three friends looking to win a slew of fabulous prizes by defeating Sam Thompson, Chelsee Healey, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Lianne Sanderson

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: Live World Athletics Indoor Championships
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on the third and final day at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The womens pentathlon gets under way with the 60m hurdles, high jump and shot put, and the finals of the womens long jump and mens shot put also take place

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No Ones Ready
programme.desc: Ross is due to make an important speech at the museum, and invites the gang along for moral support - but bickering and personal problems soon threaten to make him late. Comedy, starring David Schwimmer

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe is alarmed to learn she has attracted the unwanted attentions of a stalker, and Monica comes up with a plan to deal with her broodiness. American comedy, guest starring David Arquette, with Lisa Kudrow and Courteney Cox

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Metaphorical Tunnel
programme.desc: Chandler ponders the importance of commitment, Monica starts work in a restaurant and Phoebes home truths make her an unpopular choice as Joeys agent. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Actress Isabella Rossellini makes Rosss day by dropping into Central Perk for a coffee. Phoebes efforts to introduce her half-brother to the city give him the wrong idea about her line of work. Comedy, with David Schwimmer, Lisa Kudrow and Giovanni Ribisi

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Janice persuades the pals to reveal their deepest, darkest secrets about a period in their relationships three years earlier. Phoebe is trying to move out without telling Monica, Rachel wants one last fling before she gets married and Ross makes a distressing discovery. Comedy, starring Matthew Perry and Maggie Wheeler

programme.title: Live World Athletics Indoor Championships
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on the third and final day at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The womens pentathlon concludes with the long jump and 800m, with Saga Vanninen of Finland the reigning champion after beating Irelands Kate OConnor into second place last year in China. Medals are also awarded in the womens pole vault, 1500m, 800m, 60m hurdles and 4x400m, and the mens 1500m, long jump, 800m and 4x400m

programme.title: Posh Pawn
programme.desc: Boss James Constantinou and the team appraise more items brought along by members of the public. A divorcee from Newcastle wants to sell her £185,000 necklace to pay for her childrens private-school fees, a crystal-covered drum kit used on a Kylie Minogue tour catches the eye of staff member Patrick, and a widowed pensioner hopes to make a fortune from her precious rings. Plus, a valuation doesnt come easy to the experts when a client turns up with a 7ft Terminator model

programme.title: Sam and Billie: Sister Act
programme.desc: Sam and Billie take mum Sue for a wellness check - but its the sisters who end up getting some shocking results - and Sam realises someone has been stealing from her

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: The presenter is joined by comedian and award-winning podcaster Joanne McNally, who speaks to Claudia about her record-breaking stand-up tour, Pinotphile. Bafta-nominated comedian and actor Guz Khan speaks about his new Amazon Prime series Bait, which he stars in alongside Riz Ahmed. Former One Direction star and singer-songwriter Niall Horan joins Claudia to share how he went from global boy band success to solo chart-topping artist and talk about his new album, featuring his latest single Dinner Party

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes global music star Taylor Swift, inviting her to discuss her album The Life of a Showgirl. Also appearing are Oscar-winner Cillian Murphy, who is playing the head teacher in reform school drama Steve and actors Greta Lee and Jodie Turner-Smith, who are appearing together in sci-fi reboot Tron: Ares. Plus, Domhnall Gleeson talks about his new comedy role in The Office spin-off The Paper and Lewis Capaldi performs Something in the Heavens

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: Layton realises some dreams are out of reach as the rest of the train deals with his status change. Wilford makes a staggering discovery

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: Life within a specialist clinic, where a team of top dermatologists treats a range of skin conditions for patients who often havent been able to get treatment on the NHS

programme.title: Party Animals
programme.desc: Drama following the lives of a group of twentysomething political researchers working for MPs in Westminster. Junior minister Jo Porter is humiliated by her Tory counterpart James Northcote in a debate on youth crime. She demands reasons for the debacle from her researcher Danny, whose lobbyist brother Scott is busy trying to make contacts among the Conservatives. With Raquel Cassidy and Patrick Baladi

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Claire from Glasgow, Gordon from Glossop, Elliott from South Shields and Risha from London pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
